First, I'll compute the squared distances to avoid floating-point inaccuracies. Then, check if the sum of the two smaller sides is greater than the largest side. But maybe it's easier to check all three inequalities.

Alternatively, since the squared distances are positive, I can sort the sides and check if the sum of the two smaller ones is greater than the largest. That would avoid dealing with square roots and possible precision issues.

    # Sort the sides
    sides = sorted([a, b, c])
